youtube_items.title: [YT] Six Years of NASA's Mars Curiosity Rover
[YT] NASA Astronaut Anil Menon Prelaunch News Conference (April 29, 2026)
youtube_items.summary: [music] >> Good afternoon and welcome to NASA's Johnson Space Center. I am Chelsea Byarte, here joined by our guest, NASA astronaut Anil Menon. Uh he's getting ready to launch to the International Space Station on the Soyuz MS-29 mission in July alongside his two crewmates, Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina. Uh they're not joining us here today as they're back in Star City for training. Um but Anil here is going to be taking your questions in three ways. So, we have reporters here in the room. Uh we've got reporters on our phone bridge dialed in, and we're taking some questions pre-submitted on social media as well that we're ready for. If you're joining us on the phone, please go ahead and press star one to enter the question queue. If your question has already been asked or you want to

youtube_items.title: [YT] Progress 95 Cargo Ship Docking
youtube_items.summary: [music] >> Good evening from Mission Control in Houston and the International Space Station Flight Control Room here at NASA's Johnson Space Center. Here's a look at the orbit 3 team led by flight director Diane Dailey and her team of flight controllers working in tandem with Russian flight controllers half a world away at the Russian Mission Control Center on the outskirts of Moscow. Teams are monitoring the automated arrival of the uncrewed Progress 95 spacecraft. It'll dock to the after port of the Zvezda service module here in about 45 minutes. This is the final leg of its 2-day 33 orbit journey carrying nearly 3 tons of food, fuel, and supplies to the Expedition 74 crew of seven living and working on the International Space Station. And here you see on your screen just activated

raw_items.title: NASA Kennedy Center Director Announces Plans to Retire
raw_items.summary: NASA announced Friday Janet Petro, center director for the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida, is retiring. Prior to joining NASA, Petro worked in a variety of military and industry positions, ultimately beginning her career at the agency in 2007 and working her way up to center director, as well as serving as acting administrator […]

Friedman, left, and NASA’s Artemis II crewmembers CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, and NASA astronauts Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Reid Wiseman, right, ring the closing bell of the Nasdaq market session, Thursday, April 30, 2026. NASA’s Artemis II mission took Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and […]

raw_items.title: Key Support Equipment Arrives at Kennedy for Roman Space Telescope
raw_items.summary: Technicians at NASA’s Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility (PHSF) at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida offloaded eight high-efficiency particulate air (HEPA) wall modules and other ground support equipment on April 27. The equipment will support launch processing of the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. Each 1,800-pound module enhances the PHSF’s clean room systems, helping meet […]

raw_items.title: NASA’s STORIE Mission to Tell Tale of Earth’s Ring Current
raw_items.summary: Earth’s magnetic field is like a powerful trap. It lures electrically charged particles in space, near our planet, and snares them in an invisible, doughnut-shaped pen around Earth known as the ring current. This captive swarm of charged particles plays an important role in how Earth reacts to changing conditions in space, called space weather, which […]

raw_items.title: Artemis 3 plans remain uncertain as schedule slips
raw_items.summary:
More than two months after NASA announced revised plans for the Artemis 3 mission, the agency has provided few details about the mission amid signs its schedule may be slipping.

The post

raw_items.title: NASA to increase value of CLPS contract to support surge of lunar lander missions
raw_items.summary:
NASA is planning to increase the total value of a contract for robotic lunar lander missions to support a proposed surge in flights for the agency’s moon base plans.

The post

raw_items.title: The opportunity beyond orbital data centers
raw_items.summary:
Investor attention is starting to shift toward ventures that could be enabled by orbital data centers, even as the massive computing networks proposed by SpaceX and others remain years from reality.

The post

raw_items.title: Starcloud seeks more orbital data center funding shortly after unicorn status
raw_items.summary:
Starcloud is looking to raise at least $200 million in a deal that would double the two-year-old orbital data center startup’s valuation to about $2.2 billion, a source close to the situation confirmed.

raw_items.title: SpaceX marks May Day, National Space Day with Starlink mission on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral
raw_items.summary: The Starlink 10-38 mission saw SpaceX deploy 29 broadband internet satellites into low Earth orbit. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station happened at 2:06:10 p.m. EDT (1806:10 UTC).
